description_long: This object is a subwoofer speaker unit with a rectangular, boxy form and a woodgrain finish, giving it a classic, high-fidelity audio equipment appearance. The front face features a large, circular black speaker grille, likely made of fabric or mesh, which conceals the speaker driver behind it. The rear panel is equipped with a black control panel that includes several rotary knobs for gain and crossover adjustments, labeled input/output ports for high-level and line-in connections, and various switches, all clearly marked with white text. The remaining sides are smooth and unadorned, showcasing the rich, natural wood texture. The overall construction suggests a sturdy, dense build typical of quality audio equipment, with the control panel and speaker grille as the main interactive elements. This subwoofer would commonly be found in home theaters, garages, or urban entertainment spaces.
description: A wood-finished subwoofer with a large black speaker grille on the front and a detailed control panel with knobs and ports on the back.
description_view_0: The rear view displays a black control panel with multiple knobs, switches, and labeled input/output ports set into the woodgrain surface.
description_view_1: This side view shows a plain, continuous woodgrain surface with no visible features or controls.
description_view_2: The front view highlights a large, circular black speaker grille set into the woodgrain box, with a small label in the lower corner.
description_view_3: The opposite side view is a featureless woodgrain panel, matching the other side in texture and color.
attribute_brand_logo: A small rectangular label with white text is visible on the lower left corner of the front grille; the text is not fully legible.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grille is a solid black circular area, likely fabric or mesh, with no visible geometric or decorative pattern.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel on the rear features three rotary knobs (gain, crossover, phase), several labeled switches, and multiple input/output ports arranged in a rectangular black panel with white printed labels.
